stopConditions: Stop after official NTHU or CTLD sources support narrow staged claim candidates.
Do not infer university-wide binding rules from example syllabus language.
Do not use search snippets, third-party ranking pages, vendor pages, or non-university news as policy evidence.

methodsAttempted.notes: Found the official NTHU English announcement, CTLD guideline PDFs, and several unrelated course/news/research pages.

methodsAttempted.query: site:ctld.site.nthu.edu.tw Guidelines for Collaboration Co-learning Artificial Intelligence Competencies NTHU
methodsAttempted.notes: Found the CTLD AI/digital-learning index and official CTLD-hosted AI guideline PDFs.
Traversed official CTLD index links to the Chinese AI guideline and the English course-syllabi guidance PDF; rejected one PDF whose extracted content did not provide AIGC policy evidence.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found an NTHU English announcement, an official CTLD AI/digital-learning document index, a Chinese NTHU AI collaboration and literacy guideline PDF, and an English CTLD generative-AI course-syllabi guidance PDF. Claims are staged narrowly as university guidance or course-syllabi examples, not canonical public facts.

artifacts.sourceTitle: NTHU Establishes Guidelines for AI in Education

artifacts.claimText: NTHU announced the Guidelines for Collaboration, Co-learning, and Cultivation of Artificial Intelligence Competencies in University Education on May 1, 2023 in response to generative AI tools including ChatGPT.

citation.citationTitle: National Tsing Hua University - NTHU AI Policy Tracker record
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: NTHU Establishes Guidelines for AI in Education
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: In response to the advent of generative artificial intelligence (AI) chatbot ChatGPT, National Tsing Hua University (NTHU) in Taiwan announced on May 1st the Guidelines for Collaboration, Co-learning, and Cultivation of Artificial Intelligence Competencies in University Education.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: NTHU says it announced the Guidelines for Collaboration, Co-learning, and Cultivation of Artificial Intelligence Competencies in University Education on May 1 in response to ChatGPT and generative AI.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: NTHU Establishes Guidelines for AI in Education
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow staged source-status claim directly supported by an official NTHU source. This is not marked canonical or human-reviewed.

artifacts.claimText: NTHU's Chinese AI collaboration and literacy guideline recommends that faculty and students use AI in educational settings transparently and responsibly, with instructors clearly stating course AI rules and both students and instructors honestly disclosing AI use when relevant.

citation.citationTitle: National Tsing Hua University - NTHU AI Policy Tracker record
citation.publisher: National Tsing Hua University Center for Teaching and Learning Development
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 國立清華大學 大學教育場域 AI 協作、共學與素養培養指引
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 我們建議本校師生在教育場域中使用各種人工智慧(artificial intelligence，以下簡稱AI，不限於生成式人工智能)產品時應抱持透明與負責的態度：透明：教師應明確指出課程對於使用AI的規範，學生與教師使用AI時應誠實揭露。負責：教師與學生應理解AI僅能作為素材提供的來源之一。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The Chinese guideline recommends that NTHU faculty and students use AI transparently and responsibly, with instructors stating course AI rules and both students and instructors disclosing AI use when relevant.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: 國立清華大學 大學教育場域 AI 協作、共學與素養培養指引
citation.publisher: National Tsing Hua University Center for Teaching and Learning Development
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow staged teaching-guidance claim. The source is a recommendation/guideline, not a canonical public rule.

artifacts.claimText: NTHU's Chinese AI collaboration and literacy guideline says faculty and students should be able to check the accuracy of AI-generated content and take responsibility for content they produce.

citation.citationTitle: National Tsing Hua University - NTHU AI Policy Tracker record
citation.publisher: National Tsing Hua University Center for Teaching and Learning Development
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 國立清華大學 大學教育場域 AI 協作、共學與素養培養指引
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 負責：教師與學生應理解AI僅能作為素材提供的來源之一。使用時應具備檢核其正確性的判讀能力，並對自己產生的內容負責。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The guideline says faculty and students should treat AI as one source of material, check accuracy, and take responsibility for their own produced content.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.citationTitle: 國立清華大學 大學教育場域 AI 協作、共學與素養培養指引

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow staged academic-integrity-adjacent claim directly supported by the Chinese official guideline.

artifacts.claimText: For educator teaching, NTHU's Chinese AI guideline says the university respects instructors' course AI-tool strategies and that instructors should state student AI-use rules in syllabi, especially correct AI citation and disclosure of the use process.

citation.citationTitle: National Tsing Hua University - NTHU AI Policy Tracker record
citation.publisher: National Tsing Hua University Center for Teaching and Learning Development
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 國立清華大學 大學教育場域 AI 協作、共學與素養培養指引
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 教師教學 R 校方尊重教師對於課程使用 AI 工具的策略，教師應於課程大綱中敘明學生 使用 AI 的規則，尤其是正確引註 AI 並揭露使用的歷程。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: For educator teaching, the guideline says NTHU respects instructors' AI-tool strategies and that instructors should state student AI-use rules in syllabi, especially correct citation and disclosure.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow staged claim about instructor syllabus guidance; not generalized beyond the official NTHU guideline wording.

artifacts.claimText: NTHU's Chinese AI guideline gives assessment recommendations: depending on the course, AI assistance may be allowed for assignments, but diverse submission formats are recommended; exam questions should emphasize deeper reasoning, creativity, analysis, and critical thinking.

citation.citationTitle: National Tsing Hua University - NTHU AI Policy Tracker record
citation.publisher: National Tsing Hua University Center for Teaching and Learning Development
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 國立清華大學 大學教育場域 AI 協作、共學與素養培養指引
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 學習評量（作業/報告） R 依據課程屬性，可同意使用 AI 協助答題，但建議以多元形式繳交作業... 學習評量（考試） R 出題方向建議以更多需要深層推理、創造力、分析... 批判的問題，而非單純引述規則性問題。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The guideline recommends diverse assignment formats if AI assistance is allowed, and says exams should emphasize deeper reasoning, creativity, analysis, and critical thinking.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow staged teaching/assessment guidance claim. The evidence is recommendation language, not a binding assessment rule.

artifacts.claimText: NTHU's Chinese AI guideline describes AI competence as broader than programming and says NTHU plans to cultivate student AI competence through formal and informal learning, including foundational and specialized AI courses and integrating AI into courses across disciplines.

citation.citationTitle: National Tsing Hua University - NTHU AI Policy Tracker record
citation.publisher: National Tsing Hua University Center for Teaching and Learning Development
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: 國立清華大學 大學教育場域 AI 協作、共學與素養培養指引
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: AI 素養不僅是關涉撰寫程式的能力... 本校規劃培養學生 AI 素養：統整正式（課程、研究）與非正式的學習（社團、活動、競賽、實習）... 提供基礎及專業的 AI 課程... 將 AI 融入課程。
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The guideline frames AI competence as more than programming and says NTHU plans formal and informal AI-competence development through courses, research, activities, competitions, internships, foundational/specialized courses, and AI integration across curricula.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow staged claim about NTHU's AI competence planning described in the official Chinese guideline.

artifacts.claimText: NTHU CTLD's course-syllabi generative-AI guidance encourages faculty members to specify course logistics and AIGC policies using four options: unrestricted use with no disclosure required, conditionally open, prohibited use, or not applicable.

citation.citationTitle: National Tsing Hua University - NTHU AI Policy Tracker record
citation.publisher: National Tsing Hua University Center for Teaching and Learning Development
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Integrating Ethical Guidelines for Generative AI into NTHU Course Syllabi
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Faculty members are encouraged to specify course logistics and policies regarding AIGC using one of the following four options: 1. Unrestricted use with no disclosure required 2. Conditionally open; please specify how to utilize generative AI in course output 3. Prohibited use; please specify relevant monitoring mechanisms 4. Not applicable
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The CTLD syllabi guide encourages faculty to choose among four AIGC policy options: unrestricted without disclosure, conditionally open, prohibited use, or not applicable.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: AIGC course logistics and policy options
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow staged course-syllabi guidance claim. It describes encouraged options, not a single required university-wide AI rule.

artifacts.claimText: In CTLD's example ethics statements for conditionally open generative-AI use, undisclosed usage may allow instructors, the institution, or relevant units to reevaluate an assignment or report or withhold scores.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: If usage is discovered without proper disclosure, instructors, the institution, or relevant units have the right to reevaluate the assignment or report or withhold scores.

artifacts.notes: Approved only as a claim about CTLD example ethics statement language; not generalized into a binding university-wide sanction policy.

artifacts.title: National Tsing Hua University - staged AI policy evidence
artifacts.summary: Staged official-source evidence for NTHU found university guidance on AI transparency, responsibility, course AI-use rules, assessment design, AI competence cultivation, and CTLD examples for course-syllabi AIGC ethics statements. Claims remain agent-reviewed candidates and are not canonical or human-reviewed.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The CTLD course-syllabi PDF provides encouraged options and examples; related claims are scoped to course-syllabi guidance and example statements.
The Chinese AI guideline uses recommendation language; claims should not be read as a final canonical university-wide enforcement rule without human review.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
